keynotes.text: A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. CEPA. (Allium Cepa) Natural order.- Liliaceae. Common name.- Onion. General Analysis.- Acts especially upon the conjunctiva and the respiratory mucous membrane, producing a highly irritated condition, greatly resembling an acute catarrh. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Allium cepa) Very melancholy. (Ign., Natr. mur., Nux m .) Head.- (Allium cepa) Confusion of the head. Headache, with coryza ; in the room, evenings ; better in open air ; aggravated on returning to a warm room. ( Puls .) Pains in temples, most in right ; aggravated by winking, extending over forehead, worse on left side. Eyes.- (Allium cepa) Excessive non-excoriating (excoriating, Euphr.) lachrymation ; left eye worse, with redness of the eyeball ; sensitive to light (Acon., Bell.) ; worse evenings. Itching, biting, burning in the eyes. Swelling of lids around the eyes. (Ars) Nose.- (Allium cepa) Copious watery . discharge from the nose, and watering of eyes. (Acon., Euphr.) Constant sneezing, with - profuse acrid (bland, Euphr .) coryza ( Ailanth. gland. Aur ., Merc., Sang., Squilla) ; when coming into a warm room. (In cool air, Ars .) Ichor oozing out of nose ; second stage of scarlatina. (Ailanth. gland., Arum.) Fluent coryza, headache, lachrymation, cough, heat, thirst, trembling of hands ; worse in evening and in a room ; better in open air. ( Puls .) Throat.- (Allium cepa) Pain in throat extending to the ear. (Kali bi.) Stomach.- (Allium cepa) Canine hunger. Pressure in stomach. Pain in region of pylorus. Abdomen.- (Allium cepa) Rumbling in bowels. Very offensive flatus. Urinary Organs.- (Allium cepa) Frequent and copious urination. (Apis, Arg. met., Arg. nit., Phos. ac.) Urine red, with much urging and burning in the urethra. (Acon., Canth .) Respiratory Organs.- (Allium cepa) Catarrhal hoarseness. (Arum, Caust ., Carb. v., Phos .) Tickling in throat, with aching in larynx. Hacking cough from inhaling cold air . (Acon.) Constant inclination to hack. Violent catarrhal laryngitis ; the hoarse cough seemed to split and tear the larynx. Oppressed breathing from pressure in middle of chest. Limbs.- (Allium cepa) Sore, tired feeling of the limbs, especially arms. Generalities.- (Allium cepa) Stitches and burnings ; aching. Flitting heat over whole body, and thirst. Weak and tired ; has to lie down. Coldness alternates with heat during catarrh. Sweats easily and copiously. Aching throughout the body. Neuralgic pains, like a long thread, in face, head, neck, and elsewhere ; worse evenings. Skin.- (Allium cepa) Pricking as from pins. Redness ; nettlerash. Aggravation.- (Allium cepa) Prominently y in the evening and in a warm room. Better in the open air. Therapeutic Range.- (Allium cepa) Inflammation and increased secretions of the mucous membranes ; coryza ; acute catarrh ; measles. Compare.- (Allium cepa) Acon ., Caust., Euphr., Ipec., Phos., Puls. Antidotes.- (Allium cepa) Arn ., Cham., Verat. alb. Copyright © Robert Séror 2003
